channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Crimson Desert Sales Hit 5m in Under a Month
item.content:encoded:
Hugely ambitious open-world action RPG Crimson Desert has managed to soar past yet another sales milestone, and it's taken the game less than a month to achieve that feat.

At the start of the month, developer and publisher Pearl Abyss revealed that Crimson Desert had passed four million sales, and now, the game has cracked the five million barrier, according to a post on the official Crimson Desert X account.

In general, Crimson Desert has had a very good time of it when it comes to sales. The game reached two million sales in just a handful of hours, going on to hit the three million mark just a few days after that. Sales are slowing down just a little, then, but Pearl Abyss has no cause to worry yet.

If you're unfamiliar with Crimson Desert, it's a huge action RPG created by Black Desert Online developer Pearl Abyss, although it contains just about every other genre under the sun as well.

Originally revealed back in 2020, Crimson Desert's development cycle has been a fairly long and protracted one; it was delayed indefinitely in 2021 before eventually re-emerging with a release window of late 2025, although that was pushed back to early 2026.

The game sees protagonist Kliff trying to put the fragmented Greymanes faction back together after a catastrophic event scatters its members to the four winds, although it's got lots of other story strands to follow as well.

In addition to exploration and combat, Crimson Desert also offers a host of MMORPG-style side activities and diversions, including numerous sidequests, fishing, hunting, and crafting, among many others.

If you want to see what all the fuss is about, you can check out Crimson Desert right now on PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S (it's pretty technically demanding, so don't expect it to come to Switch 2 anytime soon).

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
